steps.title: Identify what must be illustrated
steps.content: The claim is that Mrs. Spring Fragrance demonstrates her concern for what's happening at her home in Portland while she is away. A qualifying quotation must show her actively thinking about home — giving instructions or expressing worry about things left behind, not about activities during her trip.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — "My honorable cousin is preparing for the Fifth Moon Festival, and wishes me to compound for the occasion some American 'fudge,' for which delectable sweet, made by my clumsy hands, you have sometimes shown a slight prejudice.": Focuses on preparations for an upcoming festival, not on concerns for what is happening at home while she is away. ✗
  • Choice B — "Next week I accompany Ah Oi to the beauteous town of San José. There will we be met by the son of the Illustrious Teacher.": Describes an upcoming event during her trip; nothing about home in Portland. ✗
  • Choice C — "Forget not to care for the cat, the birds, and the flowers. Do not eat too quickly nor fan too vigorously now that the weather is warming.": Direct instructions about household care — the cat, birds, flowers, and her husband's health — showing concern for what is happening at home. ✓
  • Choice D — "I am enjoying a most agreeable visit, and American friends, as also our own, strive benevolently for the accomplishment of my pleasure.": Describes her enjoyment of the trip; no concern for home. ✗
That matches Choice C — "Forget not to care for the cat, the birds, and the flowers. Do not eat too quickly nor fan too vigorously now that the weather is warming.".
